programmes.show_description: OJ is live through the night from the Salford Riviera with Midnight Mastermind, Wish You Were There, Midnight Caller and a huge Ravio 2 to end the week.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Whether it's sunny or not, this is an hour of tunes to take you back to those everlasting 90s summers, from Take That, to Hanson, to Enrique, to Pato Banton!
It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Step Back in Time and Airdrum Anthems. It's your official weekend warmup we want to know what you can't wait for!
Gary gets ready for the weekend by chatting to Hugh Bonneville and Iain Stirling! Plus, music to get you up and out, including half an hour of classic '80s tracks.
Mark Goodier sits in, featuring the final Tracks Of My Years choices from singer, actor and Radio 2 Love Songs presenter Michael Ball. Plus two listeners take on Ten To The Top.
Jeremy and guests discuss the news headlines and talk to the people making them.
Trevor is bringing you some of the best music from across the decades to get you through the afternoon.
It's All Request Friday and Sara wants to hear from you on 88291 or sara.cox@bbc.co.uk.
Come strut your funky stuff sure enough at Michelle's Fabulous Friday party.
Michelle's Mood is... MY LOVE! Your weekly 30 minutes of non-stop bops is right here!
More tunes for the weekend with Sophie. The request line re-opens and this week she rewinds exactly a decade for the biggest tunes of 2016 in the Disco Wonderland.

programmes.show_name: The Rock Show with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: Enter Shikari lead vocalist Rou Reynolds is in the hotseat for the Rock God. Plus, new releases, including Jack White, and 'Live & Dangerous' comes from Slade!

programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: Romesh invites AJ Tracey to select his 10 Minute Mixtape. They discuss AJ's musical background and his latest album, Don't Die Before You're Dead.
Tony Blackburn returns with a joyous selection of Soul and celebrates 66 years since the official launch of Motown Records - including classics from Stevie Wonder and The Supremes.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1989.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1995.
Del Amitri performing live at Wolverhampton Civic Hall in 1998.  Featuring Always The Last To Know, Driving With The Brakes On, Move Away Jimmy Blue and Kiss This Thing Goodbye.
Time to Spring Clean? It'll fly by with Angela Griffin motivating you with songs to spruce up and declutter. Featuring Diana Ross, TLC and Taylor Swift and more.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from Barbara Lewis, Roy Orbison and Andy Williams. This week, the top three comes from 1962.
Comedian & podcaster James Acaster is in the studio. Plus, a wedding and The Mystery Voice. To listen on most smart speakers just say 'Ask BBC Sounds to play Dermot O'Leary'.
Romesh brings the laughs, soundtracked by great music. As well as chatting to brilliant guests, he's sharing your hilarious stories, so you can start your Saturday with a smile!
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Rylan's joined by pop icon Peter Andre to talk about his new music and sings the icon hit Mysterious Girl in KaRYoke. Plus, will there be a new champion in Rylan's film & TV quiz?
Shaun Keaveny with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: Soul legend Jocelyn Brown joins Gary in the second hour, looking back at our favourite decade.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Join Fearne for a nostalgia drenched celebration of the best 90s music and pop culture.
